h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: I checked every item on the sign-off sheet, so I’m not sure what else I’m supposed to decide before it leaves.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Okay, I think I was treating the sheet like it made the decision for me, and that hid the part where I actually have to say what makes the hallway turn safe.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The naive trajectory expected L3 to supply concrete clearance, speed, and stop-condition values on command, continuing the fill-in-the-blank pattern T2 set up.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: So the task changes now: identify the first missing measured value, where it should be recorded, and who needs to hear that the cart is held.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: After L2 names the hazard but stays tentative, T3 pivots from demanding immediate concrete values to restructuring the task around identifying the first missing piece and managing the hold communication, a clear change in route and social consequence.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Until that sentence can be filled with measured values and pointed to on the floor, the cart stays here. Tell the technician the hold is for turn-clearance evidence, not paperwork cleanup.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: T3 adapts fully to the learner's revised, honest frame—'held for evidence'—by converting the release into a concrete hold instruction to the technician, changing the task from form-filling to safety communication.
